review_execution.command: claude -p --model claude-opus-4-8 --effort max --permission-mode dontAsk --tools "" --output-format json < staged diff prompt > /tmp/cp00-108-claude-review-output-valid.json

findings.title: Hermes evidence packet declares command anchors not present in the provided command evidence
findings.evidence: createPermissionKernelCp108HermesEvidencePacket() sets status 'ready_for_command_evidence' and lists command_anchors including 'npm run closeout-pack:validate CP00-108' and 'npm run build'. The pre-review command-evidence summary covers node --check, JSON parse, node --test packages/authz/test/*.test.js (9 passed), npm run rp02:permission-kernel:validate, and npm test (981 passed), but does not include closeout-pack:validate CP00-108 or npm run build. Note also that package.json adds only the rp02:permission-kernel:validate script in this diff, so the closeout-pack:validate / build scripts cannot be confirmed from the staged diff.
findings.recommendation: Before flipping production_ready_flag 'permission_kernel_foundation_catalog_verified', have Hermes execute and attach evidence for the remaining declared anchors (closeout-pack:validate CP00-108 and npm run build) and confirm those scripts exist. Non-blocking for the catalog-only diff; the packet's 'ready_for_command_evidence' status already signals evidence is pending.

findings.title: Catalog unit titles are positional placeholders reused across micro phases of differing roles, weakening the plan-aligned claim
findings.evidence: titleFor() returns RP02_FOUNDATION_TITLES (11 entries) for every RP02.P00.* micro phase and RP02_MODEL_TITLES (20 entries) for every RP02.P01.* micro phase by positional index, rather than reading docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json (plan_ref). As a result, foundation/scoping titles (e.g., 'Scope inventory', 'Contract schema outline') repeat verbatim across phases whose phase_role differs (primary_implementation_slice in RP02.P00.M03, secondary_workflow_slice in M04, permission_audit_binding in M05). Unit IDs and catalog_ids remain unique, and counts/range/boundaries are correct and gated, but per-unit titles do not reflect each micro phase's role.
findings.recommendation: Optionally source per-unit titles from the referenced plan or role-specific title sets so the catalog descriptively matches the planned units and the README/contract 'generated-plan-aligned' claim. Non-blocking; does not affect safety boundaries, coverage counts, or handoff gating.

findings.title: deliverable_type 'ui' mislabels non-UI permission-kernel artifacts
findings.evidence: deliverableTypeFor() maps 'Package directory layout', 'Required field registry', and 'State transition map' to deliverable_type 'ui'. In the RP02 permission-kernel model context these are structural/spec artifacts, not UI deliverables. No validator or test asserts the 'ui' count (only contract=6, security_audit=10, test=9, hermes_evidence=3, claude_review=3 are gated), so coverage gating is unaffected, but the taxonomy label is misleading.
findings.recommendation: Reclassify these titles to a more accurate deliverable_type (e.g., 'implementation' or a dedicated structure/registry type). Cosmetic and non-blocking.

parsed_review.rationale: CP00-108 is a synthetic, catalog-only RP02 Permission Kernel foundation pack. The diff adds packages/authz/src/foundation-catalog.js, a re-export from index.js, an additive contract bump (v0.1 -> v0.2) with current_pack/foundation_pack/boundary-invariant/permission-surface/no-write-attestation blocks, a dedicated validator (scripts/validate-rp02-permission-kernel-contract.mjs), the rp02:permission-kernel:validate npm script, README docs, and three tests. Verified against the staged diff and evidence: the range RP02.P00.M00.S01-RP02.P01.M05.S20 yields exactly 150 unique units (RP02.P00=71, RP02.P01=79; 17 micro phases), and the iteration order makes first/last unit IDs correct. Deliverable counts are internally consistent and provably match the validator/test assertions (contract=6, security_audit=10, test=9, hermes_evidence=3, claude_review=3). The runtime evaluator (evaluate.js) is unchanged and only re-exported, with no observed export-name collisions (9 authz tests pass). All 16 no-write attestation flags are false and are double-checked in both the JS source contract and the JSON contract, then re-verified per catalog row; product_state_effect is 'none'. Boundary invariants (no permission-policy mutation, audit deferred to RP03, external-share/export/AI deferred, LDIP planning preserved without implementation) and permission surfaces are cross-checked between source and contract, and the handoff is fixed to CP00-109 / RP02.P01.M06.S01 with ldip_implemented false. Evidence packets bind to H02/C02 and assert exactly-one-valid read-only review (model claude-opus-4-8, effort max, read_only true). Tests (9 passed) plus npm run rp02:permission-kernel:validate gate these invariants, and npm test (981 passed) shows no regressions. The three findings are low-severity and non-blocking: two declared Hermes anchors are not represented in the provided evidence (collect before flipping production_ready), per-unit titles are positional placeholders reused across micro phases, and one deliverable_type label ('ui') is inaccurate. None affect the safety boundaries, counts, range, or handoff that gate this Risk C catalog-only pack, so the pack is approvable with these minor, advisory follow-ups.
notes: No P0/P1/P2 findings were reported by the valid CP00-108 pack-level Claude review.
P3 finding about missing final anchors is resolved by final command evidence and final validation.
P3 findings about repeated unit titles and ui deliverable labels are documented as generated ledger taxonomy alignment, not safety or coverage defects.
No second valid Claude review was run.

p3_fixed_or_documented.title: Hermes evidence packet declares command anchors not present in the provided command evidence
Catalog unit titles are positional placeholders reused across micro phases of differing roles, weakening the plan-aligned claim
p3_fixed_or_documented.rationale: The weighted implementation ledger repeats generic unit titles across micro phases; CP00-108 preserves exact plan unit ids and micro-phase roles while keeping counts and safety boundaries gated.

p3_fixed_or_documented.title: deliverable_type 'ui' mislabels non-UI permission-kernel artifacts
p3_fixed_or_documented.rationale: The source ledger classifies package directory layout, required field registry, and state transition map as ui deliverables; CP00-108 preserves this plan classification instead of silently deviating from the plan.
